claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
circumstances: Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£10.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
Child in local authority care for 12 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

Let's start with: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have a child. I'm inquiring about my son Alex - he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. So you have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old.

Now I need to ask: **Do you live in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. 

Next question: **Does Alex live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't live with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask: **Do you make regular financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, pocket money, or towards his accommodation)?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do help out with some money - I contribute towards his clothes and things like that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To properly assess your eligibility, I need to understand the amount you're contributing.

**How much do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep, and how often do you make these contributions (weekly, monthly, or as a lump sum)?**

For reference, the current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child (approximately £110 per month). You would need to be contributing at least this amount to qualify for Child Benefit when your child lives with someone else.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute £10 a week towards Alex's upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. Let me ask one more important question:

**Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, his mum claims it and he lives with her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Eligibility Assessment for Alex:**

Unfortunately, you are **not eligible** for Child Benefit for Alex. Here's why:

1. **Only one person can claim:** Alex's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for him, and only one person can receive Child Benefit for the same child.

2. **Contribution amount is insufficient:** Although you do contribute towards Alex's upkeep, you're contributing £10 per week. When a child lives with someone else, you need to contribute at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment to qualify - which is currently £25.60 per week for an eldest or only child. Your contribution of £10 per week falls short of this requirement.

Since Alex's mother is already receiving Child Benefit and you don't meet the contribution threshold, you cannot claim Child Benefit for Alex at this time.

If your circumstances change in the future (for example, if you increase your contributions to at least £25.60 per week and Alex's mother stops claiming), you would need to contact the Child Benefit Office to discuss your situation.

reasonings: Alex is 8 years old (under 16), so he meets the age requirement. The claimant lives in the UK, which meets the residency requirement. However, Alex does not live with the claimant - he lives with his mother. While the claimant does contribute towards Alex's upkeep, the contribution is only £10 per week. According to the rules, when a child lives with someone else, you can only continue to get Child Benefit if you contribute towards the child's upkeep by at least the same amount as the Child Benefit payment. The current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child. Since £10 per week is less than £25.60 per week, the claimant does not meet the contribution threshold. Additionally, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and Alex's mother is already claiming it. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has one child, Alex (age 8), who lives with his mother. While the claimant makes contributions towards Alex's upkeep (£10 per week), this falls short of the minimum required amount (£25.60 per week) to qualify for Child Benefit when the child lives with someone else. Additionally, Alex's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for him, and only one person can claim for the same child. The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

The current Child Benefit rate is £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child. Since £10 per week is less than £25.60 per week, the claimant does not meet the contribution threshold. Additionally, only one person can claim Child Benefit for a child, and Alex's mother is already claiming it. Therefore, the claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

While the claimant makes contributions towards Alex's upkeep (£10 per week), this falls short of the minimum required amount (£25.60 per week) to qualify for Child Benefit when the child lives with someone else. Additionally, Alex's mother is already claiming Child Benefit for him, and only one person can claim for the same child. The claimant is not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
